info.description:
### Query transactions charged under a subscription

This service allows merchants to retrieve the full details of a subscription alongside
the list of transactions that have been charged against it, filtered by a date range
and an optional result size limit.


<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### To keep in mind
>
> This endpoint requires your **Private Key** (`Private-Merchant-Id`) for authentication.
> Keep this credential secure and never expose it on client-side code.

## How it works

Send a `GET` request with the target `subscriptionId` as a path parameter. Use the
optional query parameters to scope the results to a specific date window and control
the number of transactions returned.

The response contains two main sections:

- **Subscription details** — static metadata about the subscription (card info, plan,
  periodicity, contact details, etc.)
- **Transactions array** — the list of charges executed under that subscription within
  the requested date range.

## Using the API

* The current version of the Kushki API rests at: `https://api.kushkipagos.com/`
* The testing version rests at: `https://api-uat.kushkipagos.com/`

## Endpoints

* [Get subscription transactions](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/subscription-transactions/operations/get-data-v1-subscription)

[Return Home](/)

> Got a suggestion on this documentation? [Contact Us.](mailto:techwriting@kushki.com)

post.description:
> #### This functionality is available for the following models: > > ☑ Acquirer
> ☑ Aggregator Request a card token that can later be used to charge a customer using the `charge` endpoint.

Important to consider: If you are using this option to request the token, be sure to comply with all the PCI requirements to handle card data on your servers.

> #### Token expiration > >Tokens will expire in 30 minutes and can be used only for a single transaction (even if the transaction was wrong). You will need to request a new token when it expires. > **Note: The token delivered by Kushki only encrypts and sends information. If you want to store the card information for future purchases, go to the [One-Click & Scheduled Payments](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/one-click-and-scheduled-payments) section.**
### Recurring transactions* > #### This functionality is available for the following models: > > ☑ Acquirer
> ☐ Aggregator Send the `transactionMode` parameter for request a recurring charge `token` that can later be used to create a recurring charge as follows: - **initialRecurrence**: Merchants must generate a token by sending complete card information (number, expiration date, cvv, etc) to register it. A token will be returned to continue the payment. - **subsequentRecurrence**: The merchant will be able to generate a token by sending the data of the previously sent card with an `initialRecurrence`, with this the merchant will be able to omit the entry of the CVV and make a charge later with the current flow. > **NOTE**: If you want to process a recurring charge with an external engine, you must skip sending the CVV.
### Do you want to validate an account?* > #### This functionality is available for the following models: > > ☑ Acquirer
> ☐ Aggregator Send the `transactionMode` parameter as `accountValidation ` for request an account validation token that can be later used to verify an account using the [card/v1/validation](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/card/operations/create-a-card-v-1-validation) endpoint. Make sure you set the totalAmount to `0`.
### 3D Secure (3DS) authentication* To authenticate a card through 3D Secure, in addition to the information required to generate a token, the fields listed below must be submitted | **PROPERTY** | **POSSIBLE VALUES** | **DESCRIPTION** | |----------------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | authValidation | `url` `iframe` | Define the desired integration type for 3DS authentication. Use `url` for redirection or `iframe `for embedding. | | callbackUrl | string | Callback where the 3D Secure authentication response will be sent. | If the transaction triggers a 3D Secure authentication rule on the merchant, in addition to the token, the fields listed below will be returned | **PROPERTY** | **POSSIBLE VALUES** | **DESCRIPTION** | |---------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------------| | url | string | URL to be used for 3D Secure authentication. | | secureService | `3dsecure` | Indicates the transaction authentication service. | | secureId | string | Security ID for the transaction. | **Example** ```json { "token": "sBkQ7F110000tI1HVq116862fd5Ah3mG", "url": "https://uat-auth.kushkipagos.com?token=sBkQ7F110000tI1HVq116862fd5Ah3mG&merchantId=306cb10581bb4acb9a2bfc77e163c482&bin=NDM0OTAwMzA=&callbackUrl=https://www.kushkipagos.com/callback/&isSandbox=false", "secureService": "3dsecure", "secureId": "1f5584db-0c5b-c729-a19c-6eb0283ca448" } ``` In the `url` field will be the url that will need to be displayed to the cardholder for 3D Secure authentication, if necessary. For more detailed information, visit the [3D Secure API authentication](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/card-payments/3d-secure/3ds-via-api) guide. For more information about 3D Secure, see [Accept payments with 3DS](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/card-payments/3d-secure/3ds-overview). > **NOTE:** To enable 3DS in test mode in your merchant, it is required to have an additional configuration performed by Kushki. #### callbackUrl The callback function will let you know if the 3D Secure authentication was successful or if there were any problems during the process. This url will be called automatically after authenticating, through a 301 redirect by making a GET request to the url sent in the `callbackUrl` field, sending the parameters of the response in the url (path parameters). > **Note**: The boolean parameter sucess will indicate whether the transaction was successfully authenticated (`true`) or if there was a problem with 3D Secure authentication (`false`). If it is `true`, you can proceed to make the charge. If `false`, the cardholder will have up to 3 attempts to retry authentication before the transaction is declined. | **PROPERTY** | **TYPE** | **DESCRIPTION** | |--------------|----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | success | boolean | Indicates whether 3D Secure authentication was successful or not. | | token | string | Token returned by Kushki to be used when making a charge in case 3D Secure authentication has been successful. | | message | string | In case there is a problem during authentication, the reason for the problem will be returned here. | ##### Example of a successful authentication callback ``` https://www.tutienda.com/?success=true&token=cfa0bfec88324bd7a5c6c1ad9135a846 ``` ##### Example of a callback with authentication problem ``` https://www.tutienda.com/?success=false&message=Error%20en%20la%20validación%20de%203DS&token=cfa0bfec88324bd7a5c6c1ad9135a846 ```

post.summary: Make a charge or deferred charge
post.description:
<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☑ Aggregator

Make a charge with a `token` provided by Kushki which represents the customer's credit card.


### Deferred charges 

Deferred transactions vary depending on the affiliation model:

#### For Acquiring

For deferred charges in **Colombian** merchants, you have to set the `deferred` object that includes the `creditType`, `graceMonths`, and `months` parameters.

In all countries, `graceMonths` must be sent with the value of **`00`**.

### Example: Credit Type `01` (Colombia's Standard Installments)

```json
  "deferred": {
    "graceMonths": "00",
    "creditType": "01",
    "months": 6
  }
```

#### For Aggregator model

For deferred charges in Colombian merchants in aggregator model, you can send the number of installments in the `months` parameter.
 ```
 "months": 3,
  ```
<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Important!
>
> In any case, you should consult the request deferred options method as these vary according to the BIN of the card.

### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?*

Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request.



> **Endpoint time out: 29 sec**

### Do you have your own subscriptions engine?*

<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☐ Aggregator


In case you have your own subscriptions engine (**only PCI Compliance merchants**), you can follow the steps below to process recurring charges:

1. Obtain a [card token](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/card%2Foperations%2Fcreate-a-card-v-1-token#recurring-transactions) by sending `initialRecurrence` in the `transactionMode` parameter.
2. Use the obtained token in step 1 to make an initial charge (see the example named _Initial recurrence charge for subscriptions_).
3. Make sure you save the **transactionReference** number of the charge completed in step 2.
4. For the subsequent transactions, obtain a [card token](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/card%2Foperations%2Fcreate-a-card-v-1-token#recurring-transactions) by sending `subsequentRecurrence ` in the `transactionMode` parameter.
5. Make a charge by sending the **transactionReference** number in the `initialRecurrenceReference` field (see the example named _Subsequent recurrence charge for subscriptions_)

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: application/json

initialRecurrenceReference.description: Send here the `transactionReference` number obtained in the charge request of your initial recurrence charge without CVV. To make a charge of this type, you must have obtained previously a card token by sending the `subsequentRecurrence` parameter.

creditType.description:
The deferred option code. Common values for Colombia:
* `01`: Cuotas (Standard Installments)
* `02`: Cuotas con período de gracia (Installments with grace period)

Send the value returned in the `type` field of the Request Deferred Options response.

months.description: Number of installment months. Valid values are returned by the Request Deferred Options endpoint.

ignoreWarnings.description: Set this parameter to `true` if you want to continue with the charge despite the warning rules

externalSubscriptionID.description: An alphanumeric identifier for an external subscription managed outside Kushki. When this field is provided, the transaction is flagged as a recurring payment initiated by a third-party subscription engine. This ensures correct reporting to issuers and improves authorization rates. Click [here](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/en/recurring-payments/external-subscriptions) for more information.

externalReferenceId.description: Transaction ID generated from the merchant side. **The only special characters accepted in this field are `-` and `_`**.

originalTransactionID.description:
The unique identifier of the original transaction, used exclusively in **external subscription flows** where Kushki processes on-demand charges and tokenization is handled externally.

* **Condition**: Must be sent if the `externalSubscriptionID` field is present.

#### Network-Specific Rules
| Card Network | Validation Rule |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Visa** | Exactly 15 numeric digits. |
| **Mastercard (MC)** | Alphanumeric value between 1 and 22 characters. |

> **Note**
> Ensure the value matches the exact format required by the processor to avoid transaction errors.

capabilities.description:
Include the `franchiseTransactionCode` parameter to request the **Transaction Identifier (TID)** assigned by the payment networks (e.g., Visa, Mastercard). When this value is sent in the array, the response will include the `transactionIdentifier` field within the `network` object, which is primarily used for transaction reconciliation with the franchises
```json
"capabilities": [
    "franchiseTransactionCode"
  ]
```

post.description:
Make a single charge without the need for a card token.

<!-- theme: danger -->
> #### This is an On-demand service only available for PCI DSS compliance companies.

<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☐ Aggregator

### Limitations
- Only available for Visa and Mastercard.
- Not compatible with Siftscience nor TransUnion antifraud tools.
- Not compatible with Kushki 3DS authentication tool (Use instead your own 3DS authentication engine).
- Not compatible with Kushki OTP authentication.


### Deferred charges 

For Chile, Colombia and Peru, deferred transactions vary depending on the affiliation model.

#### For Acquiring

For deferred charges in **Colombian** merchants, you have to set the `deferred` object that includes the `creditType`, `graceMonths`, and `months` parameters.

In all countries, `graceMonths` must be sent with the value of **`00`**.

| Country | Deferred Payment Type | `creditType` Value |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |

### Example: Credit Type `01` (Colombia's Standard Installments)

```json
  "deferred": {
    "graceMonths": "00",
    "creditType": "01",
    "months": 6
  }
```

#### For Aggregator model

For deferred charges in Colombian merchants in aggregator model, you can send the number of installments in the `months` parameter.
 ```json
 "months": 3,
  ```

### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?*

Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request.


### Do you have your own subscriptions engine?*

In case you have your own subscriptions engine (**only PCI Compliance merchants**), you can follow the steps below to process recurring charges:

1. Send `initialRecurrence` in the `transactionMode` parameter and process the charge. For this initial transaction, you must send the CVV.
3. Make sure you save the **transactionReference** number of the charge completed in step 1.
4. For the subsequent transactions, send `subsequentRecurrence ` in the `transactionMode` parameter and include the **transactionReference** number in the `initialRecurrenceReference` field. Now you can omit the CVV.
>An alternative option to process external subscriptions is by sending the parameter `externalSubscriptionID`. Click [here](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/en/recurring-payments/external-subscriptions#1-recurring-transactions-without-cvv-with-external-subscription-engine) for more information.

delete.description:
<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☑ Aggregator

Void a transaction identified by its `ticketNumber`. The `ticketNumber` is provided when a charge or a deferred charge is successful.
When the date of the transaction to void surpasses the timeframe in order to automatically void a transaction, this one turns into a `refund`.

<hr>

### Partial voids

For partial voids it is necessary to send the `amount` object, otherwise is not required.

#### Partial void request example

```json
{
  "fullResponse": "v2",
  "amount": {
    "subtotalIva": 500,
    "subtotalIva0": 0,
    "ice": 0,
    "iva": 0,
    "currency": "COP"
  }
}
```

<hr>

    "subtotalIva0": 9976,
    "ice": 2099,
    "iva": 8967,

<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☑ Aggregator

Returns an object with deferred options available for your customers. The deferred options are shown depending on the credit card `bin`. The `bin` corresponds to the **first six or eight digits** of the credit card number.

<!-- theme: danger -->
> This service is only available for credit cards
>

parameters.name: Public-Merchant-Id
parameters.description: In your Kushki Console, this value is labeled as **Public Key** and can be found within the **Credentials** section.

name.description: The descriptive name of the credit option.

type.description:
**Summary of `type` Field Values by Country**

|`type` Value | Description of Type | Notes on Installment Types |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `all` | All available types | **Issuer Installments** (interest may apply). |

months.description: The number of months available to offer customers. Examples: For Chile (Issuer Installments), Colombia, and Peru, this may range from 2 to 48. For Chile (Merchant Installments without interest), it will be 2 to 12.

monthsOfGrace.description: The available grace months (if applicable). This will be an empty array `[]` if not applicable.

post.description:
<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☑ Aggregator

Use this endpoint to request an authorization using a `token` provided by Kushki.

You authorize a card payment when you want to verify that sufficient credit exists on a customer's credit card before processing a sale. The cardholder's credit limit will be reduced by the amount of the authorization until the authorization is captured or expires.

- The authorization will expire after 28 days for credit cards and after 7 days for debit cards from the time of request.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Product in beta version 🔐👨‍💻
>
> We are working on our beta version. Stay tuned for its official release! You can also contact your account manager for more information.

**NOTE**: An authorization can be canceled through the [Void a transaction](../reference/CARD.yaml/paths/~1v1~1charges~1{ticketNumber}/delete) endpoint. After performing the cancellation, no reauthorizations can be made on the canceled transaction.

post.description:
Make a preauthorization (two-step payment) without the need for a card token.

<!-- theme: danger -->
> #### This is an On-demand service only available for PCI DSS compliance companies.

You preauthorize a card payment when you want to verify that sufficient credit exists on a customer's credit card before processing a sale. The cardholder's credit limit will be reduced by the amount of the authorization until the authorization is captured or expires.

- The authorization will expire after 28 days for credit cards and after 7 days for debit cards from the time of request.

<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☐ Aggregator

### Limitations
- Only available for Visa and Mastercard.
- Not compatible with Siftscience nor TransUnion antifraud tools.
- Not compatible with Kushki 3DS authentication tool (Use instead your own 3DS authentication engine).
- Not compatible with Kushki OTP authentication.

**NOTE**: An authorization can be canceled through the [Void a transaction](../reference/CARD.yaml/paths/~1v1~1charges~1{ticketNumber}/delete) endpoint. After performing the cancellation, no reauthorizations can be made on the canceled transaction.

### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?*

Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request.

### Do you have your own subscriptions engine?*

In case you have your own subscriptions engine (**only PCI Compliance merchants**), you can follow the steps below to process recurring charges:

1. Send `initialRecurrence` in the `transactionMode` parameter and process the preauthorization. For this initial transaction, you must send the CVV.
3. Make sure you save the **transactionReference** number of the charge completed in step 1.
4. For the subsequent transactions, send `subsequentRecurrence ` in the `transactionMode` parameter and include the **transactionReference** number in the `initialRecurrenceReference` field. Now you can omit the CVV.
>An alternative option to process external subscriptions is by sending the parameter `externalSubscriptionID`. Click [here](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/en/recurring-payments/external-subscriptions#1-recurring-transactions-without-cvv-with-external-subscription-engine) for more information.

post.description:
<!-- theme: none -->
> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☑ Aggregator

Create a recurring charge with a `token` provided by Kushki which represents the customer's credit card.

### Payment Execution

Kushki processes __recurring charges to the registered card automatically__. Payments will be executed and repeated according to the amount and period indicated in the subscription.

The billing logic starts running every day __from 6am GMT-5__. Make sure that the subscription was created before this time if you want the first payment to be executed on the same day of registration. Alternatively, your customer can register his/her card some days in advance, before Kushki executes the first charge. When creating a subscription, you must indicate the __start date `startDate`__.

#### Retries

If a payment is rejected, Kushki has a retry logic to try to recover the charge. By default, __there are 3 retries for 3 days in a row from the payment day originally defined in `startDate`__. For example, for a monthly subscription with start date: `10-01-2021`, with a rejection on `10-02-2021`, payment will be retried until `13-02-2021`, 3 times each day, for a total of 9 payment attempts.

You can modify the retry days for a subscription and also define specific charging days.

If the __last charge attempt was declined__, we may send you a notification via WebHook informing you about it. In these cases, we recommend you to contact the card owner and offer him/her a set of alternatives for paying the fee that could not be charged; for example, changing the registered credit card and generating a [charge on demand](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/one-click-and-scheduled-payments%2Foperations%2Fcreate-a-subscription-v-1-card-1) only for one time. Kushki will continue executing automatic charges over the following periods.

##### Customize retry logic

You can customize the collection retry logic by submitting the `retryConfiguration` object as follows:

1. `retryType`:"schedule". If you choose schedule, you can specify in the `value` field you can specify in `value` how often the collection will be reattempted.
In the next example, the collection will be reattempted 3 times per day every 2 days for the entire month.
```json
{
   "retryConfiguration":{
      "retryType":"schedule",
      "value":[2]
   }
}
```

2. `retryType`:"fixed". Send the specific days of the month to retry. Ex. 10th, 20th, 30th.
  In the following example, the charge will only be reattempted 3 times a day, on the 10th, 20th and 30th of the corresponding month.
 ```json
{
   "retryConfiguration":{
      "retryType":"fixed",
      "value":[10,20,30]
   }
}
```
>Send the `retryType` as `fixed` if you do not want to retry collection for the entire month.
If the __last charge attempt was declined__, we may send you a notification via WebHook informing you about it. In these cases, we recommend you to contact the card owner and offer them a set of alternatives for paying the fee that could not be charged; for example, changing the registered credit card and generating an [One-click payment](https://docs.kushki.com/mx/en/one-time-payments/card/on-demand) only for one time. Kushki will continue executing automatic charges over the following periods.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Important!
>
> When customizing retries it should be noted that once the first collection is initiated, if it fails and retries are initiated, any updates to retries will be applied until the next subscription period (weekly, monthly, yearly).

#### Periodicity

You can choose the frequency of recurring charges by submitting any of these values in the required `periodicity` field:

* **daily**: every 1 day.
* **weekly**: every 7 days.
* **biweekly**: every 15 days.
* **monthly**: every 30 days.
* **threefortnights**: every six weeks or 42 days.
* **bimonthly**: every 60 days.
* **quarterly**: every 90 days.
* **fourmonths**: every 120 days.
* **halfyearly**: every 180 days
* **yearly**: every 360 days.
* **custom**: for [one click payments](https://api-docs.kushkipagos.com/docs/online-payments/one-click-and-scheduled-payments%2Foperations%2Fcreate-a-subscription-v-1-card-1).

### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?


Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request.

> #### This functionality is available for the following models:
>
> ☑ Acquirer</br>
> ☑ Aggregator

Make an *One-click payment* on an existing recurring charge identified by its `subscriptionId` that was created in the **Create a recurring charge** endpoint.

### Do you have your own 3DS authentication engine?*


Make sure you include the `threeDomainSecure` object in your request.


### Deferred charges 

Deferred transactions vary depending on the affiliation model:

#### For Acquiring

For deferred charges in **Colombian** merchants, you have to set the `deferred` object that includes the `creditType`, `graceMonths`, and `months` parameters.

In all countries, `graceMonths` must be sent with the value of **`00`**.

### Example: Credit Type `01` (Colombia's Standard Installments)

```json
  "deferred": {
    "graceMonths": "00",
    "creditType": "01",
    "months": 6
  }
```

#### For Aggregator model

For deferred charges in Colombian merchants in aggregator model, you can send the number of installments in the `months` parameter.
 ```
 "months": 3,
  ```
<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Important!
>
> In any case, you should consult the request deferred options method as these vary according to the BIN of the card.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of chargebacks associated with the authenticated merchant.

#### Filters object
Use the `filters` object to narrow results by `transaction_date`, `request_date`, `chargeback_status`, `country_name`, `chargeback_type`, `ticket_code`, or `chargeback_ticket_code`.

#### Time Filter
The `time` filter is required and works as follows:
 - Send either `transaction_date` **or** `request_date`
 - Sending both returns an error.
 - The maximum allowed date window is **3 months**.
#### Fields array
The `fields` array allows you to request additional fields beyond the default response set. If `fields` is omitted, only the standard fields are returned.

> **Limit:** The maximum allowed value for `page_size` in this version is **100 records** per page.

post.description:
Initiates the asynchronous generation of a chargeback export file.

Upon receiving the request, the system immediately returns a `200 OK` response with a unique `id`. Once the file has been generated and stored, Kushki sends a `POST` notification to each URL configured in the `webhooks` array, including the download link and its expiration timestamp.

The request body follows the same structure as `POST /data/v1/chargebacks/search`, with the addition of the `webhooks` field.

> **Note:** The download URL is an S3 pre-signed URL valid for **4 hours** from the moment of generation. Check `expiration_timestamp` in the webhook notification before attempting the download.

#### Webhook security

To verify that a notification was sent by Kushki and not a third party, validate the following headers included in every webhook call:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-Kushki-Id` | Unix timestamp (13 digits, milliseconds) of when the notification was sent. |
| `X-Kushki-Signature` | HMAC SHA256 signature of `private-merchant-id\|request\|X-Kushki-Id`. The signing secret is the merchant's own `private-merchant-id`. |

To validate the signature on your side, compute:

```
HMAC-SHA256({private-merchant-id}, "{private-merchant-id}|{request}|{X-Kushki-Id}")
```

And compare the result with the value received in `X-Kushki-Signature`. If they match, the notification is authentic.

post.description:
A Transfer Out `token` is the first step to start a transaction.

> #### Two transfer modes
>
> **Bank account (ACH):** Use `accountType` values `CC` or `CA` along with `bankId`, `accountNumber`, `documentType`, `documentNumber`, and `name`. Requires prior bank list consultation.
>
> **Payment key (Bre-B):** Use `accountType` values `KI`, `KP`, `KE`, `KA`, or `KM` along with `accountNumber` (in the format that matches the key type). `documentType`, `documentNumber`, `bankId`, and `name` are **optional**. The response includes a `keyResolution` object with the recipient's key value and name, which must be shown to the user for confirmation before initializing the transaction. Maximum amount per transaction: equivalent to 1,000 UVB (Unidades de Valor Básico, updated annually by the Ministry of Finance and Public Credit). Individual banks and users may set lower daily or per-transaction limits.
<!-- theme: danger -->
> #### Token expiration
>
>Tokens will expire in 30 minutes and can be used only for a single transaction (even if the transaction was wrong). You will need to request a new token when it expires.

accountType.description:
**Bank account types (ACH):**
* CC = Cuenta Corriente in Colombia.
* CA = Cuenta Ahorros in Colombia.

**Payment key types (Bre-B — Colombia 🇨🇴 only, Bre-B processor required):**
These values are exclusive to Colombian merchants (`country=CO`) with the Bre-B processor configured on their MID. Colombian merchants using a different processor, or merchants from other countries, will receive a `400` error if they send these values.
For the format and character constraints that apply to `accountNumber` for each key type, see the `accountNumber` field description below.
* KI = National identification number.
* KP = Mobile phone number.
* KE = Email address.
* KA = Alphanumeric alias.
* KM = Merchant code.

accountNumber.description:
**Colombia 🇨🇴 — Bre-B payment key formats (`accountType`: KI, KP, KE, KA, KM):**

Format is validated server-side before any request is sent to the payment network (fail-fast). An invalid format returns `400` immediately.

| `accountType` | Type | `accountNumber` format |
|---|---|---|
| `KI` | National identification number | Uppercase alphanumeric characters. Minimum 1 character. Spaces and special characters are not allowed. |
| `KP` | Mobile phone number | Exactly **10 digits**. Must start with `3`, followed by exactly 9 digits. |
| `KE` | Email address | Valid email address. 1–30 characters before `@`, 1–61 characters after `@`. Must contain a valid domain. |
| `KA` | Alphanumeric alias | Must start with `@`. Followed by uppercase alphanumeric characters only. No spaces or special characters. |
| `KM` | Merchant code | Exactly **10 digits**. Must start with `00`, followed by exactly 8 digits. |

bankId.description:
Identifier of the destination bank. Required for ACH bank account transfers. See the Get Bank List endpoint to retrieve the available codes for your merchant.

- Optional in Colombia 🇨🇴 if the transfer is by cellphone number (`accountType=NC`).
- **Not required** in Colombia 🇨🇴 when using Bre-B payment key `accountType` values (`KI`, `KP`, `KE`, `KA`, `KM`). All Colombian banks support key-based transfers by regulation.
-

currency.description: The currency for Colombia is **COP** (Colombian Peso).

name.description:
Full name of the recipient.

> **Colombia 🇨🇴 — Bre-B:** Optional. The recipient's name is returned in the `keyResolution.ownerName` field of the response, resolved directly from the payment key.

mail.description: Customer's email address. Optional for Colombia.

keyResolution.description: **Colombia 🇨🇴 — Bre-B only.** Returned when `accountType` is one of `KI`, `KP`, `KE`, `KA`, `KM`. Contains the resolved information of the payment key. This object must be shown to the user or operator for confirmation before initializing the transaction.

keyValue.description: The exact value of the payment key used to identify the recipient, as resolved by the payment network. Returned without transformation.

ownerName.description: Name of the payment key holder as resolved by the payment network. Returned **masked** (e.g., `M**** G**** L****`). The masking is applied by the payment network and cannot be modified.

post.description:
Create a smartLink that can later be shared to a customer in order to complete the payment process.

In the form you can include 6 different type of items:
1) Input: Useful to create tags so customers can include name, email and other important information for the business.
2) Select: Useful to select from a list of available options.
3) Date: Shows a calendar if you need the customer to select a specific date.
4) Area: Useful to enter multiple lines of text.
5) Checkbox: Useful to let a customer select one or more options from a limited list of choices.
6) Radio: Allows the customer to choose only one of a limited list of choices.

> Got a suggestion on this documentation? [Contact Us.](mailto:techwriting@kushki.com)

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of transactions for a specific merchant using the **Get Transaction List v2 API**. This version supports both card-present and card-not-present transactions, as well as filtering by pay-ins and pay-outs. Transactions are returned in descending order, with the most recent ones appearing first.

>  **To Keep in mind:**
>*   Transactions can only be queried through this endpoint `90 seconds` after their creation.
>*   The maximum number of transactions you can query per request is `600`.
>* 	In case the `limit` parameter is not included in the request, the maximum number of transations that will be returned is `100`.

acquirer_bank.description: Acquiring bank with whom the transaction was approved

approval_code.description: Authorization number that responds to the applicable entity for each payment method.

bank_name.description: Only for `transfer` payment method. The name of the bank from which the money transfer was made.

bin_card.description: Only for `card` payment method. Identification of the issuing bank of the card with which the transaction was made.

buy_order.description: Only for `card` payment method. Order number of the transaction. Information sent by the merchant in the transaction.

card_country.description: Only for `card` payment method. Country of the card with which the transaction was processed

card_holder_name.description: Only for `card` payment method. Name that the end customer enters as the owner of the card.

card_type.description: Only for `card` payment method. Type of card used for the transaction. The `prepaid` value is only returned for Visa and Mastercard cards processed through Kushki Acquirer.

complete.description: Date on which the transaction was completed or when it takes its final status

created.description: Displays the date on which the transaction was created

credential_metadata.description: Displays the metadata that the customer sends in the request.

credit_type.description: Only for `card `payment method. Type of deferral with which the transaction was processed.

foreign_card.description: Only for `card `payment method. Indicates if the transaction was made with a foreign card.

ice_value.description:
Special Consumption Tax that applies to certain trade transactions carried out only in Ecuador.
Note: For other countries this field is blank or with a value of 0.

last_four_digits.description: Only for `card `payment method. Last 4 digits of the card with which the transaction was made

masked_account_number.description: Only for `transfer` payment method. Displays the destination account (masked) of Payouts transactions.

merchant_id.description: Indicates the identification of the merchant

number_of_months.description: Only for `card `payment method. Displays the number of months for which payment of the transaction was deferred.

payment_brand.description: Only for `card` payment method. Displays the brand of the card with which the transaction was made.

payment_description.description: Description of the transaction that the customer indicates when sending the request to Kushki.

pin.description: Only for `cash` payment method. Payment PIN that was generated for the transaction

processor_code.description: Code of the response generated by the authorizer.

processor_id.description: transaction processor code. It is only displayed if the transaction is approved or rejected by the processor.

processor_message.description: Message of the response generated by the authorizer.

processor_name.description: name of the transaction processor. It is only displayed if the transaction is approved or rejected by the processor.

processor_type.description: Indicates whether the transaction has passed through the aggregator or gateway model according to the configuration. Only information for `SALE` type is shown.

public_credential_id.description: Public key information associated with the merchant.

recap.description: Only for `card` payment method. Authorization code provided by the processors

request_amount.description: Displays the total amount the customer requested to return (card) or the amount of the transaction (cash or transfer). In partial voids the value is different from the `request_amount` field

response_code.description: Displays the response code sent by the processor

response_text.description: Displays the response sent by the processor

retry.description: Only for `card` payment method. It is used for scheduled subscriptions, for when you cannot collect and retry collection on different days.

retry_count.description: Only for `card` payment method. Count of collection reattempts that have been made on your scheduled subscriptions.

sale_approval_code.description: Displays the sale approval number that was generated for the transaction.

sale_ticket_number.description: Displays the number of the sales receipt that was generated for the transaction.

sale_transaction_type.description: Used only for voids and refunds transactions. Indicates the `transaction_type` of the sale on which the void or refund was performed.

string_taxes.description:
Only for `card` payment method. Refers to additional taxes that apply to the transaction in Colombia.
For example: airport taxes, etc.

subscription_id.description: Only for `card` payment method. Identifier of the applied subscription.

subscription_metadata.description: Only for `card` payment method. Displays the metadata that the customer sends in the request.

subtotal_iva.description: Amount of the requested transaction excluding VAT. Information will only be displayed in this field if data was sent in this field of the request. Otherwise, it will show the value 0. Applies to approved and rejected transactions.

subtotal_iva0.description: amount of the requested transaction (equal to the amount collected). Information will only be displayed in this field if data was sent in this field of the request. Otherwise, it will show the value 0. Applies to approved and rejected transactions.

ticket_number.description: Unique numeric identifier of the transactions that apply to each of the payment methods: Cash, Transfer and Card.

total_amount.description:
1. For `VOID` or `REFUND`, shows the total amount that the customer requested to be refunded.
2. For the `SALE` case, shows the amount the customer requested to collect.

transaction_arn.description: Acquirer Reference Number: unique ID per credit card transaction under Kushki Acquirer model. This parameter may take 1 business day to be reflected.

transaction_id.description: Displays a unique identifier for all transaction types. This identifier is used to identify a transaction in Kushki.

transaction_reference.description: Unique alphanumeric identifier for all transaction types. This identifier is used to identify a transaction in Kushki

void_ticket_number.description: Displays the voucher number that was generated for the void transaction.

external_reference_id.description: Only for card transactions. Transaction ID generated from the merchant side. It must be a unique ID per transaction.

pos_friendly_name.description: Friendly display name of the terminal. Only returned in card present transactions if sent in the request.

pos_serial.description: Device serial number. Only returned in card present transactions if sent in the request.

pos_type.description: Physical device type. Possible values: `COUNTERTOP`, `MPOS`, `SMARTPOS`, `SELF_SERVICE`, `OTHER`. Only returned in card present transactions if sent in the request.

pos_connectivity.description: Network connectivity type. Possible values: `GPRS`, `WIFI`, `ETHERNET`, `DIAL_UP`, `OTHER`. Only returned in card present transactions if sent in the request.

post.description:
:::info Availability
This functionality is available for the following models: ☑ Acquirer ☑ Aggregator
:::

Retrieves merchant settlement records on demand. Supports two query modes:

- **By date range** — include `startDate` and `endDate` in the request body to retrieve a paginated list of settlement records for the specified period.
- **By ticket number** — include a `ticketNumber` in the request body to retrieve the settlement record associated with a specific transaction. When using this mode, include the `switch` query parameter to specify the payment channel. Returns a single record in the `data` array.

## Default Behavior (Date Range Mode)

- `page` defaults to `1`.
- `limit` defaults to `100` records per page.

## Date Range Limit

:::warning 6-Month Lookback Limit
Queries are limited to a maximum lookback of **6 months** from the current date.

Requests with a `startDate` older than 6 months will **not return results** — no error is thrown.

Make sure your `startDate` falls within the allowed window before sending the request.
:::

<!-- theme: warning -->
> ### 🚧 Beta — Early Access
>
> This API is in early access. Endpoints, parameters, and response structures
> may change without prior notice. Do not deploy to production without
> coordinating with the Kushki integration team.

Queues a print job to the target terminal's thermal printer via Cloud relay.
Returns **202 Accepted** with a `PENDING` status immediately.

The final result (`COMPLETED` or `FAILED`) is delivered via:
- **Webhook push**: supply `webhookUrl` in the request.
- **Status polling**: call `POST .../print/job_status?print_job_id={id}`.

## Command Types

The `commands` array contains polymorphic objects. The **`type`** property
(required on every object) determines its behavior. All enum values must be
sent in **UPPERCASE**.

| `type` | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `text` | Text line with configurable size, alignment, bold, italic, and underline. |
| `columns` | Multi-column row with proportional widths — ideal for item/price lines. |
| `divider` | Full-width separator: `SOLID`, `DOTTED`, or `EMPTY`. |
| `feed` | Advance paper N blank lines. |
| `space` | Insert precise pixel-level vertical whitespace. |
| `cut` | Trigger the auto-cutter. Silently ignored on cutterless terminals. |
| `image` | Print a Base64-encoded PNG/JPG — typically used for merchant logos. |
| `qr` | Generate a QR code natively in printer hardware. |
| `barcode` | Generate a CODE128 barcode natively in printer hardware. |

---

### `text` — Text Line

Prints a line or paragraph of text with full format control.
Append `\n` to the `text` field to force an explicit line break.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `text` | String | ✅ | — | Text to print. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `LEFT` | Horizontal alignment. |
| `size` | Integer | ❌ | `24` | Font size in px. Reference: `24` normal · `32` subtitle · `48` title. |
| `bold` | Boolean | ❌ | `false` | Bold. |
| `italic` | Boolean | ❌ | `false` | Italic. |
| `underline` | Boolean | ❌ | `false` | Underline. |

```json
{
  "type": "text",
  "text": "RESTAURANT EL BUEN SABOR\n",
  "align": "CENTER",
  "size": 32,
  "bold": true
}
```

---

### `columns` — Multi-Column Row

Prints text in proportional columns on a single paper line.
The `weight` field controls the relative width of each column.

| Field (per column) | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------------------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `text` | String | ✅ | — | Cell text. |
| `weight` | Integer | ❌ | `1` | Relative width. Weights `2` and `1` give the first column 2/3 of the paper. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `LEFT` | Alignment within the cell. |

```json
{
  "type": "columns",
  "columns": [
    { "text": "2x Combo Burger", "weight": 2, "align": "LEFT" },
    { "text": "$30.00",          "weight": 1, "align": "RIGHT" }
  ]
}
```

---

### `divider` — Separator Line

Prints a full-width horizontal rule. Use it to delimit receipt sections
(header, items, totals).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `dividerType` | `SOLID` \| `DOTTED` \| `EMPTY` | ❌ | `EMPTY` | Line style. |
| `offset` | Integer | ❌ | `10` | Extra space in px before the separator. |

```json
{ "type": "divider", "dividerType": "DOTTED", "offset": 10 }
```

---

### `feed` — Paper Advance

Advances the paper N blank lines. Always use before `cut` to fully expose
the printed content past the tear edge.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `lines` | Integer | ❌ | `1` | Number of blank lines to advance. |

```json
{ "type": "feed", "lines": 3 }
```

---

### `space` — Pixel-Precise Spacing

Inserts vertical whitespace with pixel precision. More granular than `feed`
for fine adjustments between receipt elements.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `pixels` | Integer | ❌ | `10` | Pixels of blank space to insert. |

```json
{ "type": "space", "pixels": 20 }
```

---

### `cut` — Paper Cut

Triggers the hardware auto-cutter. No additional parameters.
On cutterless terminals (e.g., Sunmi V2) the command is silently ignored.

```json
{ "type": "cut" }
```

---

### `image` — Base64 Image

Prints a PNG or JPG sent as Base64. Typically used for the merchant logo
in the receipt header.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `base64Image` | String | ✅ | — | Raw Base64 string. Do **not** include the `data:image/...;base64,` prefix. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `CENTER` | Image alignment. |
| `width` | Integer | ❌ | — | Max width in px. Sunmi P3 / P2 SE support up to **384 px**. |
| `algorithm` | `BINARIZATION` \| `DITHERING` | ❌ | `DITHERING` | `BINARIZATION`: maximum sharpness for logos. `DITHERING`: simulates grayscale for photos. |

<!-- theme: warning -->
> Send only the raw Base64 string. Including the `data:image/png;base64,`
> prefix will cause the image to fail silently.

```json
{
  "type": "image",
  "base64Image": "iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAo...",
  "align": "CENTER",
  "width": 384,
  "algorithm": "BINARIZATION"
}
```

---

### `qr` — QR Code

Generates a QR code natively in printer hardware from a content string —
no image rendering required. Faster and higher-density than sending a
pre-rendered bitmap.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `content` | String | ✅ | — | URL or text to encode. |
| `dotSize` | Integer | ❌ | `8` | Size in px of each QR module. Affects the total code size. |
| `errorLevel` | `L` \| `M` \| `Q` \| `H` | ❌ | `L` | Error correction: `L`=7% · `M`=15% · `Q`=25% · `H`=30%. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `CENTER` | Horizontal alignment. |

```json
{
  "type": "qr",
  "content": "https://yourstore.com/receipt/4421",
  "dotSize": 6,
  "errorLevel": "M",
  "align": "CENTER"
}
```

---

### `barcode` — CODE128 Barcode

Generates a 1D barcode natively in hardware. Format: CODE128.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `content` | String | ✅ | — | Data to encode (e.g., UPC, invoice number). |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `CENTER` | Horizontal alignment. |
| `width` | Integer | ❌ | `3` | Width of each vertical bar in points. |
| `height` | Integer | ❌ | `162` | Total barcode height in px. |

```json
{
  "type": "barcode",
  "content": "A-1234567890",
  "align": "CENTER",
  "width": 2,
  "height": 162
}
```

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Idempotency
>
> Reuse the same `printJobId` on retries to prevent duplicate prints.
> The terminal deduplicates by `printJobId`.

post.description:
<!-- theme: warning -->
> ### 🚧 Beta — Early Access
>
> This API is in early access. Endpoints, parameters, and response structures
> may change without prior notice. Do not deploy to production without
> coordinating with the Kushki integration team.

Queues a print job to the terminal's thermal printer.
Returns **202 Accepted** immediately — printing is asynchronous.

The final result (`COMPLETED` or `FAILED`) is delivered via:
- **Webhook push**: supply `webhookUrl` in the request.
- **Status polling**: call `GET /terminal/v1/print_job?print_job_id={id}`.

## Command Types

The `commands` array contains polymorphic objects. The **`type`** property
(required on every object) determines its behavior. All enum values must be
sent in **UPPERCASE**.

| `type` | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `text` | Text line with configurable size, alignment, bold, italic, and underline. |
| `columns` | Multi-column row with proportional widths — ideal for item/price lines. |
| `divider` | Full-width separator: `SOLID`, `DOTTED`, or `EMPTY`. |
| `feed` | Advance paper N blank lines. |
| `space` | Insert precise pixel-level vertical whitespace. |
| `cut` | Trigger the auto-cutter. Silently ignored on cutterless terminals. |
| `image` | Print a Base64-encoded PNG/JPG — typically used for merchant logos. |
| `qr` | Generate a QR code natively in printer hardware. |
| `barcode` | Generate a CODE128 barcode natively in printer hardware. |

---

### `text` — Text Line

Prints a line or paragraph of text with full format control.
Append `\n` to the `text` field to force an explicit line break.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `text` | String | ✅ | — | Text to print. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `LEFT` | Horizontal alignment. |
| `size` | Integer | ❌ | `24` | Font size in px. Reference: `24` normal · `32` subtitle · `48` title. |
| `bold` | Boolean | ❌ | `false` | Bold. |
| `italic` | Boolean | ❌ | `false` | Italic. |
| `underline` | Boolean | ❌ | `false` | Underline. |

```json
{
  "type": "text",
  "text": "RESTAURANT EL BUEN SABOR\n",
  "align": "CENTER",
  "size": 32,
  "bold": true
}
```

---

### `columns` — Multi-Column Row

Prints text in proportional columns on a single paper line.
The `weight` field controls the relative width of each column.

| Field (per column) | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------------------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `text` | String | ✅ | — | Cell text. |
| `weight` | Integer | ❌ | `1` | Relative width. Weights `2` and `1` give the first column 2/3 of the paper. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `LEFT` | Alignment within the cell. |

```json
{
  "type": "columns",
  "columns": [
    { "text": "2x Combo Burger", "weight": 2, "align": "LEFT" },
    { "text": "$30.00",          "weight": 1, "align": "RIGHT" }
  ]
}
```

---

### `divider` — Separator Line

Prints a full-width horizontal rule. Use it to delimit receipt sections
(header, items, totals).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `dividerType` | `SOLID` \| `DOTTED` \| `EMPTY` | ❌ | `EMPTY` | Line style. |
| `offset` | Integer | ❌ | `10` | Extra space in px before the separator. |

```json
{ "type": "divider", "dividerType": "DOTTED", "offset": 10 }
```

---

### `feed` — Paper Advance

Advances the paper N blank lines. Always use before `cut` to fully expose
the printed content past the tear edge.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `lines` | Integer | ❌ | `1` | Number of blank lines to advance. |

```json
{ "type": "feed", "lines": 3 }
```

---

### `space` — Pixel-Precise Spacing

Inserts vertical whitespace with pixel precision. More granular than `feed`
for fine adjustments between receipt elements.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `pixels` | Integer | ❌ | `10` | Pixels of blank space to insert. |

```json
{ "type": "space", "pixels": 20 }
```

---

### `cut` — Paper Cut

Triggers the hardware auto-cutter. No additional parameters.
On cutterless terminals (e.g., Sunmi V2) the command is silently ignored.

```json
{ "type": "cut" }
```

---

### `image` — Base64 Image

Prints a PNG or JPG sent as Base64. Typically used for the merchant logo
in the receipt header.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `base64Image` | String | ✅ | — | Raw Base64 string. Do **not** include the `data:image/...;base64,` prefix. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `CENTER` | Image alignment. |
| `width` | Integer | ❌ | — | Max width in px. Sunmi P3 / P2 SE support up to **384 px**. |
| `algorithm` | `BINARIZATION` \| `DITHERING` | ❌ | `DITHERING` | `BINARIZATION`: maximum sharpness for logos. `DITHERING`: simulates grayscale for photos. |

<!-- theme: warning -->
> Send only the raw Base64 string. Including the `data:image/png;base64,`
> prefix will cause the image to fail silently.

```json
{
  "type": "image",
  "base64Image": "iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAo...",
  "align": "CENTER",
  "width": 384,
  "algorithm": "BINARIZATION"
}
```

---

### `qr` — QR Code

Generates a QR code natively in printer hardware from a content string —
no image rendering required. Faster and higher-density than sending a
pre-rendered bitmap.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `content` | String | ✅ | — | URL or text to encode. |
| `dotSize` | Integer | ❌ | `8` | Size in px of each QR module. Affects the total code size. |
| `errorLevel` | `L` \| `M` \| `Q` \| `H` | ❌ | `L` | Error correction: `L`=7% · `M`=15% · `Q`=25% · `H`=30%. |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `CENTER` | Horizontal alignment. |

```json
{
  "type": "qr",
  "content": "https://yourstore.com/receipt/4421",
  "dotSize": 6,
  "errorLevel": "M",
  "align": "CENTER"
}
```

---

### `barcode` — CODE128 Barcode

Generates a 1D barcode natively in hardware. Format: CODE128.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|:--------:|---------|-------------|
| `content` | String | ✅ | — | Data to encode (e.g., UPC, invoice number). |
| `align` | `LEFT` \| `CENTER` \| `RIGHT` | ❌ | `CENTER` | Horizontal alignment. |
| `width` | Integer | ❌ | `3` | Width of each vertical bar in points. |
| `height` | Integer | ❌ | `162` | Total barcode height in px. |

```json
{
  "type": "barcode",
  "content": "A-1234567890",
  "align": "CENTER",
  "width": 2,
  "height": 162
}
```

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Idempotency
>
> Reuse the same `printJobId` on network-failure retries.
> The terminal deduplicates the job and will not print twice.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Implemented by your POS system
>
> This endpoint lives in **your infrastructure**, not in Kushki ONE.
> When a print job completes or fails, the terminal POSTs to the `webhookUrl`
> you supplied in the print job request.
> Document it here so your team knows the expected payload shape.

Your webhook endpoint must return `HTTP 2xx`. No automatic retry is
guaranteed in the current Beta. If the webhook does not arrive within ~60
seconds, use the `GET /terminal/v1/print_job` polling endpoint as a fallback.

threeDomainSecure.title: threeDomainSecure
threeDomainSecure.description:
Send this object if you have your own 3DS authentication engine.

**VISA (Required)**:
* cavv
* eci
* specificationVersion

**Mastercard (Required)**:
* directoryServerTransactionID
* eci
* ucaf
* specificationVersion
* collectionIndicator

**Electronic Commerce Indicator (ECI) allowed values:**

* Visa
  * eci: `05` and `06` represent a secure transaction.
  * eci: `07` represents a risky transaction. For processing risky transactions, also set the `acceptRisk` field to `true`. **By sending `true`, in the `acceptRisk` field, merchants will assume responsibility in case of chargebacks.**
* Mastercard:
  * eci: `01` and `02` represent a secure transaction.
  * eci: `00` represents a risky transaction. For processing risky transactions, also set the `acceptRisk` field to `true`. **By sending `true`, in the `acceptRisk` field, merchants will assume responsibility in case of chargebacks.**

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Important notice about support for version 1 of 3D Secure!
>**NOTE**: Support for 3D Secure 1.0.2 and related technology ended in October 2022. Merchants will need to migrate to version 2 of the protocol to avoid any impact on their transactions.

cavv.description: **Required only for Visa.** Cardholder Authentication Verification Value. For Visa Secure transactions, the issuer's access control generates a CAVV. **Base 64 encoded.**

eci.description: The Electronic Commerce Indicator (ECI) is a value returned by the directory servers (i.e. Visa, Mastercard, JCB and American Express) indicating the result of the attempted authentication.

specificationVersion.description:
3DS protocol version to implement

**NOTE: Support for 3D Secure 1.0.2 and related technology ended in October 2022. Merchants will need to migrate to version 2 of the protocol to avoid any impact on their transactions.**

acceptRisk.description: Indicates whether risky transactions are required to be processed or not. For processing high-risk transactions, set this parameter to `true` and send `07` in the case of Visa or `00` for Mastercard in the `eci` field. **By sending `true` in this field, merchants will assume responsibility for chargebacks.**

ucaf.description: **Required only for Mastercard.** Universal Cardholder Authentication Field. **It´s the equivalent to the CAVV. Base 64 encoded.**

collectionIndicator.description:
**Required only for Mastercard**

* 0 (for ECI 00) = 3DS authentication failed or could not be attempted, this may be due to technical problems, the user entered the authentication code wrong, etc.
* 1 (for ECI 01) = The issuer is not ready, however the transaction has a liability shift because the merchant did request 3DS.
* 2 (for ECI 02) = Transaction authenticated by the issuer with liability shift.

directoryServerTransactionID.description: **Only required for Mastercard.** Transaction ID of the 3D Secure provider's directory server. Applies only for version 2 of 3DS.

CIT=_Customer_-_Initiated Transaction_. MIT=_Merchant_-_Initiated Transactions_. Only required for Mastercard as an **informative** field when you are going **to process external subscriptions** (with `externalSubscriptionID` or `transactionMode` flows)
**CIT** - `C101` (Credential-on-file [ad hoc]) - `C102` (Standing Order [variable amount/fixed frequency]) - `C103` (Subscription [fixed amount/fixed frequency]) - `C104` (Installment) **MIT** - `M101` (Unscheduled Credential-on-file) - `M102` (Standing Order [variable amount/fixed frequency]) - `M103` (Subscription [fixed amount/fixed frequency]) - `M104` (Installment) - `M205` (Partial Shipment) - `M206` (Related/Delayed Charge) - `M207` (No-show)

ChargebackItem.description:
Represents a chargeback record. The set of fields returned depends on whether the `fields` array is included in the request.

Fields listed under **Default fields** are always returned. Fields listed under **Optional fields** are only included when explicitly requested via the `fields` parameter.

id.description: Unique identifier of the chargeback record.

chargeback_ticket_code.description: Chargeback ticket number. May contain the value `NO_INFO` when the data is not available.

merchant_name.description: Name of the merchant or branch associated with the transaction.

chargeback_status.description: Current status of the chargeback in the Kushki system.

reason_code.description: Chargeback reason code as defined by the payment network. In some cases only the description (`reason_description`) is available.

request_amount.description: Amount requested by the merchant in the original sale transaction.

currency_code.description: The currency for Colombia is **COP** (Colombian Peso).

transaction_date.description: Date and time when the original sale was executed (ISO 8601 UTC).

request_date.description: Date when the chargeback was reported to the card networks or processors.

deadline_representation_date.description: **Calculated field.** Deadline for submitting representation evidence. Calculated as `request_date + 15 calendar days`.

deadline_resolution_date.description: **Calculated field.** Maximum deadline for case resolution. Calculated as `request_date + 120 calendar days`.

risk_level.description:
**Calculated field.** Urgency level of the chargeback at query time, based on the number of days remaining until `deadline_representation_date`.

| Value | Condition | |-------|-----------| | `HIGH` | ≤ 5 days until deadline | | `MEDIUM` | 6 – 15 days until deadline | | `LOW` | > 15 days until deadline |

ticket_code.description: Sale transaction ticket number linked to this chargeback. Use this field to correlate the chargeback with its original sale in Kushki.

operation_id.description: Unique code of the chargeback request.

transaction_reference.description: Reference number of the original sale transaction.

merchant_code.description: Unique code of the merchant or branch (Merchant ID).

approved_transaction_amount.description: Approved amount of the original sale transaction.

transaction_type.description: Transaction type of the original sale. Expected value — `SALE`.

transaction_status.description: Final status of the original sale transaction.

transaction_date_source.description: Transaction date as reported by the card networks or processors.

notification_status.description: Indicates whether the chargeback notification was sent to the merchant's registered email addresses.

documentation_reception_date.description: Date when the chargeback documentation was received.

issuing_delivery_date.description: Date when the chargeback representation was initiated.

create_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (13 digits, milliseconds) of when Kushki registered the chargeback request in its system.

update_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (13 digits, milliseconds) of the last update to the chargeback record in Kushki.

processor_name.description: Name of the processor that handled the original sale transaction.

card_country_name.description: Country of the card used in the transaction.

security_service.description: Security service the sale transaction passed through (e.g., 3DS, fraud scoring).

security_message.description: Response message from the security service.

last_four_digit_code.description: Last four digits of the card used in the transaction.

source.description: Source through which the chargeback was reported to Kushki.

message.description: Technical detail of the validation error.

SettlementDateRangeRequest.title: SettlementDateRangeRequest
SettlementDateRangeRequest.description: Request body for querying settlement records by date range.

startDate.description: Start of the settlement period to query, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

endDate.description: End of the settlement period to query, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use alongside `limit` for pagination. **Default:** `1`.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. **Default:** `100`.

SettlementTicketRequest.title: SettlementTicketRequest
SettlementTicketRequest.description: Request body for querying a settlement record by ticket number. Returns a single record in the `data` array.

ticketNumber.description: Ticket number of the transaction whose settlement record you want to retrieve. This value is returned as `ticketNumber` in charge and capture responses. When this field is present, the `switch` query parameter is required.

SettlementResponse.title: SettlementResponse
SettlementResponse.description: Settlement records matching the query. When querying by `ticketNumber`, `data` contains exactly one element.

data.description: List of settlement records matching the query. Contains a single element when querying by `ticketNumber`.

pagination.description: Pagination metadata for the result set.

limit.description: Maximum number of records returned per page.

total.description: Total number of settlement records available for the queried period.

totalPages.description: Total number of pages available based on the current `limit`.

SettlementRecord.title: SettlementRecord
SettlementRecord.description: Individual settlement record for a processed transaction.

payment_date.description: Date on which the settlement payment was made to the merchant, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

day.description: Day of the month of the transaction creation date.

month.description: Month of the transaction creation date.

country.description: Country where the transaction was processed.

currency_code.description: The currency for Colombia is **COP** (Colombian Peso).

processor_type.description: Processing model under which the transaction was handled (e.g., `AGGREGATOR_FORMAL`, `ACQUIRER`).

credential_alias.description: Alias assigned to the credential used for the transaction.

created.description: Date on which the transaction was created, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

ticket_number.description: Unique ticket number assigned to the transaction by Kushki.

sale_ticket_number.description: Ticket number of the original sale transaction. Populated for voids and refunds; empty for sale transactions.

payment_method.description: Payment method used for the transaction.

buy_order.description: Merchant-provided order reference for the transaction.

document_number.description: Document number associated with the transaction, if applicable.

bin_card.description: First 6 digits of the card number (BIN).

card_type.description: Type of card used for the transaction.

foreign_card.description: Indicates whether the card was issued outside the merchant's country.

number_of_months.description: Number of deferred months applied to the transaction. `"0"` for non-deferred transactions.

issuing_bank.description: Name of the bank that issued the card.

approval_code.description: Authorization code returned by the issuer upon approval.

cash_pin.description: Cash PIN associated with the transaction, if applicable.

payment_point.description: Payment point identifier, if applicable (used for cash transactions).

min_fee_amount.description: Minimum fee amount applied to the transaction.

approved_transaction_amount.description: Total amount approved for this transaction in the transaction currency.

iva_value.description: VAT amount applied to the transaction.

ice_value.description: ICE tax amount applied to the transaction (Impuesto a Consumos Especiales).

variable_fee.description: Variable fee amount charged by Kushki for this transaction.

variable_percentage.description: Variable fee rate applied, expressed as a percentage.

static_amount.description: Fixed fee amount charged per transaction.

kushki_commission.description: Total Kushki commission amount charged for this transaction (variable fee + fixed fee).

iva_kushki_commission.description: VAT applied to the Kushki commission.

kushki_amount.description: Total amount retained by Kushki (commission + VAT on commission).

metadata.description: Additional metadata associated with the transaction, if provided at charge time.

fraud_retention.description: Amount retained for fraud prevention purposes.

adjustment.description: Manual adjustment amount applied to the settlement record.

wallet.description: Amount associated with wallet operations, if applicable.

fund_release.description: Amount released from a previously held fund.

commission_msi.description: Commission amount applied for months without interest (MSI) deferred payments.

iva_msi.description: VAT applied to the MSI commission.

pay_amount.description: Net amount to be paid to the merchant after deducting all fees and retentions (`approved_transaction_amount` minus `kushki_amount` and other deductions).

observation.description: Additional observation or note associated with the settlement record.

AmountWithTaxes.description: Amount breakdown including extra tax components (for charge and authorization).

AmountCore.description: Base amount breakdown. All values are in the terminal's configured currency.

iva.description: VAT amount. Use `0` if VAT does not apply.

subtotal_iva0.description: Subtotal exempt from VAT. Principal transaction amount.

ExtraTaxes.description: Additional tax components. Set each field to `0` if not applicable.

authCode.description: Authorization code assigned by the issuer.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (ms) when the transaction was processed.

pan.description: Masked PAN — never contains the full card number.

showReciptSignature.description: When `true`, capture a signature before printing the receipt.

tip.description: Tip amount. Use `0` if no tip applies.

LinkFailure.description:
Unified error model for all Kushki ONE Connect endpoints.
Evaluate `type` to classify the error source, then look up `code`
in the Error Catalog.

param.description: The specific field that caused a PARAMETER error.

message.example: Invalid currency: CLsP. Expected one of: [MXN, CLP, PEN, COP]

commands.description: Ordered list of print commands. Array order = physical paper order.

printJobId.description:
Client-defined idempotency ID. The terminal auto-generates a UUID if omitted.

<!-- theme: info -->
> Always provide this to prevent duplicate prints on network retries.

externalReference.description: Free-form reference for traceability. Returned as-is in webhook and status responses.

webhookUrl.description: URL that receives the async result. Must be reachable from the terminal's network.

skipIfBusy.description:
When `true`, returns `HTTP 409` immediately if the print queue is busy.

<!-- theme: danger -->
> Do **not** set to `true` for payment receipts.

text.description: Text to print. Append `\n` to force a line break.

size.description: Font size in px. Recommended: 24 normal · 32 subtitle · 48 title.

weight.description: Relative column width. Weights 2 and 1 → first column takes 2/3 of paper.

offset.description: Extra vertical space in px before the line.

lines.description: Number of blank lines to advance.

pixels.description: Vertical blank space in pixels. More granular than `feed`.

CommandCut.description: Triggers the auto-cutter. Silently ignored on cutterless terminals.

base64Image.description:
PNG or JPG encoded in pure Base64.

<!-- theme: danger -->
> Do **not** include the `data:image/...;base64,` prefix — it will cause
> the image to fail silently.

width.description: Maximum width in px. Sunmi P3 / P2 SE support up to **384 px**.

algorithm.description:
- `BINARIZATION`: maximum sharpness for logos and line art.
- `DITHERING`: simulates grayscale for photographs.

dotSize.description: Size of each QR module in px. Affects the total code size.

errorLevel.description: Error correction: L=7% · M=15% · Q=25% · H=30%.

content.description: Data to encode. Generates a CODE128 barcode.

width.description: Width of each vertical bar in points.

code.description:
| Code | Cause | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `OUT_OF_PAPER` | No paper roll | Insert new roll |
| `COVER_OPEN` | Cover open | Close cover firmly |
| `COVER_INCOMPLETE` | Cover not latched | Re-open and close |
| `PAPER_JAM` | Paper jammed | Remove manually |
| `BUSY` | Queue busy + `skipIfBusy: true` | Retry after current job |
| `PRINTER_HOT` | Head overheated | Wait 2–3 min |
| `MOTOR_HOT` | Motor overheated | Wait for cooling |
| `CUTTER_ERROR` | Cutter jammed | Technical service |
| `OFFLINE` | Module unresponsive | Restart terminal |
| `UNKNOWN_ERROR` | Unclassified error | Check logs |
| `BAD_FORMAT` | Malformed JSON/command | Fix request |

message.example: The printer is out of paper. Insert a roll and try again.

errorMessage.example: The printer is out of paper.

externalReference.description: Reference provided at enqueue time. Empty string if not provided.

StatusComponent.title: StatusComponent
StatusComponent.description: Represents a single platform component and its current operational status, along with uptime metrics for the reported date range.

name.description: Display name of the platform component.

description.description: Optional description of the component. Returns `null` when no description has been set.

status.description:
Current operational status of the component.

* `operational`: Functioning normally with no known issues.
* `degraded_performance`: Reduced performance; component remains functional.
* `partial_outage`: Some functionality unavailable for a subset of users.
* `major_outage`: Component is completely unavailable or severely impacted.

position.description: Display order of the component within the platform's status page. Lower values appear first.

group.description: Indicates whether this component acts as a group container for child components.

showcase.description: Indicates whether this component is highlighted on the public status page.

only_show_if_degraded.description: When `true`, this component is only visible on the status page if it is experiencing an incident.

start_date.description: The date from which this component started tracking uptime, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Returns an empty string if not set.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the component was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last update to this component's record.

range_start.description: Start of the reporting period for uptime metrics, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

range_end.description: End of the reporting period for uptime metrics, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

uptime_percentage.description: Uptime percentage for the component during the reporting period (`range_start` to `range_end`). Value between `0` and `100`.

major_outage.description: Number of major outage incidents recorded during the reporting period.

partial_outage.description: Number of partial outage incidents recorded during the reporting period.

headerAuthorization-1.name: Authorization
headerAuthorization-1.description:
HMAC-SHA256 signature of the **raw JSON request body**, Base64-encoded.
Key = `Business-Code`.

Compute as: `Base64( HMAC-SHA256( body, businessCode ) )`

Omit when the request has no body (e.g. `abort`).
